commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR gates Codex native control commands and bound turns to owners or `operator.admin`, passes current auth facts into inbound-claim events, updates docs, and adds regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +44, Tests +156, Docs +6. Total +206 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main consumes command-authorization-absent Codex bound messages before native execution, while the PR head moves the owner/admin denial before that guard and dispatch now supplies non-owner facts to the plugin claim.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Queue a narrow repair for the introduced bound-message denial regression; maintainer review will still be needed for the compatibility and hook API decisions after the code fix.

target.title: fix(codex): require admin for native controls
target.body:
## Summary

### What Problem This Solves

Sensitive native Codex controls did not consistently consume the owner/admin authorization facts already available to the command and bound-conversation paths.

### Why This Change Was Made

Native execution and binding mutation should follow the existing privileged-control boundary while read-only status, help, and invalid-usage responses remain available.

## Changes

- Rebase onto merged #97955 and reuse its canonical `canMutateCodexHost` owner-or-`operator.admin` predicate.
- Preserve #97955's `/codex computer-use install` authorization and documentation unchanged.
- Require current owner identity or `operator.admin` scope for native Codex execution, control, binding, and detach operations.
- Recheck current sender authority before every bound Codex turn.
- Return an explicit owner/admin denial for blocked bound turns.
- Carry current owner and Gateway-scope facts through the generic inbound-claim event.
- Document the native-control authorization boundary.
- Add regression coverage for denied side effects, owner/admin allowance, current-sender propagation, visible denial, and read-only status access.

## Validation

### Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — 3 files, 381 tests passed.
- `node scripts/check-src-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json` — no violations.
- `node scripts/check-sdk-package-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json` — no violations.
- `node scripts/check-test-helper-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json` — no violations.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...` for touched source, tests, and docs — passed.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md` — 685 files passed.
- `node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs` — 5,493 internal links checked, zero broken.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` — passed.
- Structured Codex autoreview against merged `upstream/main` — clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Existing real stdio child-process proof remains attached with its original proof SHA and current-head alignment notes.

## Notes

### User Impact

Owners and `operator.admin` callers retain native Codex control. Ordinary command-authorized senders can still use read-only/status surfaces but cannot start, mutate, detach, or drive native Codex execution. Blocked bound turns now explain the owner/admin requirement.

`pnpm docs:list` was attempted but pnpm aborted an interactive modules-directory reconciliation in the non-TTY checkout. The underlying MDX and link checks were run directly and passed.

AI-assisted change; I reviewed the implementation and validation results.
target.author: eleqtrizit

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
